alkylation reagent: 10 mM chloroacetamide for 30 min at 37°C in the dark.
alkylation concentration: 10 mM chloroacetamide for 30 min at 37°C in the dark.
cleavage agent: proteins were digested with 20 μg trypsin overnight at 37°C.
collision energy: fragmentation by CID at a normalized collision energy of 25%.
enrichment method: performed RNASeq of Lovo cells infected with Listeria for 24 hr compared to uninfected cells.
fragmentation method: fragmentation by CID at a normalized collision energy of 25%.
instrument: in line connected to a Q Exactive mass spectrometer with a NanoFlex source.
labeling: stable isotope labeling in tissue culture (SILAC) to identify ISGylated proteins.
